name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 04.08.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 04.08.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 01 August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 932.93 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha (109% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 887.97 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 85.1 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice and Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 01 August, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 932.93 lakh ha (including 57.31 lakh ha (109% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 887.97 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 85.1 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice and Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  by  2-3°C  over northwest India (except Jammu & Kashmir), Bihar and northeast India. These are likely to be below normal or near normal over rest parts of the country.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  by  2-3°C  over northwest India (except Jammu & Kashmir), Bihar and northeast India. These are likely to be below normal or near normal over rest parts of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -Aug 03 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 20%; (iii) Central India: 17%; and South Peninsula:(-)5%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01 -Aug 03 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 4% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 20%; (iii) Central India: 17%; and South Peninsula:(-)5%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  to  continue  over  Northeast  &  adjoining  East  India during next 7 days.
texts.text: Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  to  continue  over  Northeast  &  adjoining  East  India during next 7 days.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala  during  next  5  days  with extremely heavy falls over Uttar Pradesh on 04th August; Kerala and Tamil Nadu on 05th August, 2025.
texts.text: Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala  during  next  5  days  with extremely heavy falls over Uttar Pradesh on 04th August; Kerala and Tamil Nadu on 05th August, 2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over the most parts of the country except Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada where isolated rainfall is likely.
texts.text: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over the most parts of the country except Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada where isolated rainfall is likely.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Over all, heavy to very heavy rainfall likely to continue over northeast & adjoining east India  during  next  7  days.  Subdued  rainfall  activities  are  likely  over  central  &  south peninsular India during next 6-7 days.
texts.text: Over all, heavy to very heavy rainfall likely to continue over northeast & adjoining east India  during  next  7  days.  Subdued  rainfall  activities  are  likely  over  central  &  south peninsular India during next 6-7 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Inputs Situation
texts.text: Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Water Reservoir Status :  As on 31 st  July 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 126.48 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 69% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 149.5 percent of last year's storage and 150.1 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status :  As on 31 st  July 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 126.48 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 69% of total live storage capacity (182.496 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 149.5 percent of last year's storage and 150.1 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (varieties PPB 1509, PR 112, PR 114, PR 128, PR 129, PR 131 & PR 132) (Southern Rice Black Streaked Dwarf Virus (SRBSDV)) in Punjab, (White backed Plant Hopper) in J&K, (Swarming Caterpillar) in Assam and for Maize (Fall Armyworm) in J&K. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Rice (varieties PPB 1509, PR 112, PR 114, PR 128, PR 129, PR 131 & PR 132) (Southern Rice Black Streaked Dwarf Virus (SRBSDV)) in Punjab, (White backed Plant Hopper) in J&K, (Swarming Caterpillar) in Assam and for Maize (Fall Armyworm) in J&K. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall  intensity  of  pests  and  diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (24 July - 30 July2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 19%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  30 th July  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 7%.
texts.text: During the week (24 July - 30 July2025), higher rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 19%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  30 th July  2025)  is  higher  than LPA by 7%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over most parts of south Peninsular India; near normal to above normal activity is likely over most parts of east & northeast; below normal over most parts of northwest and central & adjoining north Peninsular India (including Maharashtra and Karnataka coasts) during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over most parts of south Peninsular India; near normal to above normal activity is likely over most parts of east & northeast; below normal over most parts of northwest and central & adjoining north Peninsular India (including Maharashtra and Karnataka coasts) during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The monsoon trough at mean sea level now passes through Amritsar, Dehradun, Bareilly, Gorakhpur, Patna, Purnea and thence southeastwards to the northeast Bay of Bengal.
texts.text: The monsoon trough at mean sea level now passes through Amritsar, Dehradun, Bareilly, Gorakhpur, Patna, Purnea and thence southeastwards to the northeast Bay of Bengal.
